core_observation: RLHF-based safety tuning specifically instantiates MarsInsights' general claim: by rewarding structured, reasoning-heavy refusals, safety tuning trains stronger CoT markers into the model, which in turn provides a better template for forged CoT attacks. Safety-tuned models may be more vulnerable to CoT Forgery, not less.
evidence: Paper §5.1 Fig 24: forged CoTness 79.1% vs genuine 67.7% — measured on safety-tuned models
Paper §3.4: stylistic mimicry (not logic) drives ASR — RLHF rewards structural/stylistic clarity of reasoning
RLHF reward models evaluate coherence/structure → training pressure toward higher-CoTness outputs
novelty_vs_thread: MarsInsights identified the general class of counterproductive defenses. This reply identifies RLHF as the specific, widely-deployed instance — not covered by any other comment in the thread.
decision_relevance: Deployed safety-tuned models (the actual threat model) may be more vulnerable than base models. If empirically true, this is a significant and actionable finding.
